author: assistant
conditions: Implementation must remain confined to the shared vocabulary module, additive audit output extensions, severity reclassification, fixtures, focused tests, documentation, and self-model records.
The ok field must remain boolean with unchanged computation: false unless verification_outcome is verified_current; ok=null is prohibited.
All existing audit finding codes and messages must remain intact; no removals, renames, or repurposing.

Classification of content-stale findings must be deterministic from context captured at emission time; retroactive inference is prohibited; if determinism cannot be achieved with one code, split the emission site into distinct code variants.
The shared vocabulary module must import from neither self_model.py nor any sidecar implementation; both must import from it, and the module must contain no auditor-specific classification tables.

The severity reclassification of evidence-unavailable findings from error to warn is an acknowledged intentional behavior change; documentation must advise consumers to gate on ok or verification_outcome rather than error counts by finding code, and no findings may be suppressed in unverifiable contexts.
Both the index audit and the registry audit must adopt the vocabulary in this slice, verified by fixtures for each of the three outcomes and the mixed case.
The current repository audit must still report ok true and verification_outcome verified_current after implementation.
GAP-0004 must remain open until a separate completion verification and completion warrant exist.
Any scope expansion beyond this warrant requires a separate proposed and admitted plan.
reason: PLAN-20260719-0001 responds to GAP-0004 with the additive three-valued verification outcome vocabulary that COMP-0028 provider consensus and SYNCOMP-0016 synthesis recommended. The COMP-0029 admission review returned unanimous admit_with_warning from both providers, and all four convergent revision requests (mixed-case rollup criterion, registry-audit success criteria, content-stale emission-context determinism constraint, operator-guidance criterion) were incorporated into the revised plan at commit c8ab347510563a2b86af7875a8d17077558974b3 before this admission. The ok field's fail-closed semantics are preserved and the shared module prevents the sidecar contract (PLAN-20260717-0001) from inventing a divergent vocabulary.
scope: Admit only the implementation described by revised PLAN-20260719-0001: a shared verification-outcome vocabulary module (ai_lab/documentation/verification_outcome.py) defining the three-valued outcome enum (verified_current, stale, unverifiable), per-finding evidence classification (drift, evidence_unavailable, other), and deterministic rollup; additive extension of audit_self_model_index and audit_self_model_registry output with verification_outcome, optional evidence_status, and per-finding classification fields; severity reclassification of evidence-unavailable findings from error to warn with deterministic emission-context classification of content-stale findings; controlled fixtures and focused tests for all three outcomes, the mixed case, and both audits; and consumer-facing documentation of the severity change. No CLI behavior changes, no provider or context-selection changes, no sidecar staleness implementation, no finding-code removals or renames, no automatic remediation, and no GAP-0004 closure are admitted.
